macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Chip Selloff Deepens as AI Rally Faces Sustainability Concerns
items.summary: US stock index futures slid Friday amid a deepening chip selloff, with semiconductor stocks heading toward their steepest weekly decline in over a year, sparking fears the AI-driven surge may be unsustainable.

items.title: Fed Rate-Hike Voices Swell Ahead of July Decision Despite Inflation Cooling
items.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack joined policymakers arguing rates may need to rise to combat persistent inflation, even as June CPI fell 0.4% monthly—the largest drop since April 2020—while core inflation remains elevated at 2.6%.

items.title: Oil Hits 1-Month High Near $85 as US-Iran Tensions Escalate
items.summary: Brent crude nears $85/barrel amid renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran, with Iran threatening retaliation if US targets critical infrastructure, driving US gas prices higher.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company at $4.88T
items.summary: Apple regained the top spot with a $4.88 trillion valuation after Nvidia dropped 3.5% in market value, reversing their earlier ranking swap.

items.title: US Imposes 25% Tariffs on Some Brazilian Imports Over 'Unfair' Trade Practices
items.summary: The US announced new 25% tariffs on select Brazilian goods after a yearlong investigation concluded Brazil engaged in unfair practices, though beef, coffee, and rare-earth materials were exempted.

items.title: Netflix Weak Forecast Adds Pressure to US Stock Futures Amid Earnings Season
items.summary: A weak forecast from Netflix compounded Friday's market pressure as US corporate earnings season heats up, with Alphabet and Intel set to report next week on AI trade implications.

items.title: China's Q2 GDP Slows to 4.3% Below Target as Economic Growth Uneven
items.summary: China's economic growth fell to 4.3% y/y in Q2 from 5.0% in Q1, missing the government's 4.5%-5.0% target and Bloomberg's 4.5% median estimate, with benchmark lending rates expected unchanged for a 14th month.

items.title: HDFC Bank Q1 Profit Rises 5% Meeting Estimates on Consumer Lending Pick-Up
items.summary: India's largest private lender HDFC Bank reported a 5% first-quarter profit increase broadly meeting analyst estimates, driven by higher consumer lending and lower bad loan provisions.

items.title: SpaceX Drops 5.43% as Stock Remains Below IPO Price Amid Market Correction Fears
items.summary: SpaceX fell 5.43% in the past 24 hours, staying below its IPO price as mid-term market correction risks rise and capital flows rotate out of recent rally winners.

items.title: Amazon's Zoox Recalls Self-Driving Vehicles Over Emergency Response Issues
items.summary: Amazon's autonomous vehicle unit Zoox recalled its self-driving vehicles after reports that they impeded emergency response efforts, marking another setback for the company's AV ambitions.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: France Orders ISPs to Block Polymarket Amid Regulatory Concerns
items.summary: France has mandated internet service providers to block access to Polymarket, citing addictive mechanics, lack of self-exclusion tools, and widespread user bypassing of financial restrictions.

items.title: US Regulators Miss GENIUS Act Deadline for Final Stablecoin Rules
items.summary: One year after the GENIUS Act was signed into law by President Trump, US regulators have failed to meet the deadline for issuing final stablecoin regulations, heightening regulatory uncertainty.

items.title: Bitcoin Reclaims $67,000 While Altcoins Lead Market Losses
items.summary: Bitcoin recovered to trade above $67,000 (down ~1% in 24 hours), while Solana, Ethereum, and XRP led losses among top cryptocurrencies; the total market cap fell to $2.38 trillion with $460 million in leveraged bets wiped out.

items.title: Uniswap Governance Votes on Activating Protocol Fees for v4 Pools
items.summary: On July 19, Uniswap governance is conducting final on-chain votes to enable protocol fees for v4 pools across seven chains and for v2/v3 fees on Robinhood Chain, potentially increasing UNI token burns.

items.title: FTX to Distribute $900 Million to Creditors in Fifth Payout Wave
items.summary: FTX announced it will distribute roughly $900 million to creditors starting July 31 as part of its fifth wave of payouts from the collapsed exchange's bankruptcy proceedings.

items.title: Bitcoin Japan Taps EVO Fund for $60 Million Raise to Buy Bitcoin
items.summary: Bitcoin Japan, a company currently holding no bitcoin, has secured EVO Fund as a partner for a planned $60 million raise intended to finally purchase bitcoin for its treasury.

items.title: Fidelity Launches Own Stablecoin 'FIDD' on Crypto Exchanges
items.summary: Fidelity is launching its proprietary stablecoin, FIDD, which will be available on Fidelity's platform and major crypto exchanges, marking a significant entry for the traditional finance giant into digital assets.

items.title: Franklin Templeton Expands BENJI Tokenized Fund to BNB Chain
items.summary: Franklin Templeton has extended its $1.5 billion BENJI tokenized money market fund to BNB Chain, expanding one of the world's largest tokenized investment products to a new public blockchain.

items.title: SBI Holdings Acquires Majority Stake in Singapore Exchange Coinhako
items.summary: Japanese financial group SBI Holdings has completed the acquisition of a majority stake in Singapore-based cryptocurrency exchange Coinhako, advancing its digital asset strategy across Asia.

items.title: MicroStrategy's Saylor Opposes BIP 110, Advocates Bitcoin Neutrality
items.summary: Michael Saylor publicly opposed the BIP 110 proposal, arguing that Bitcoin must remain neutral, open, and permissionless, warning that consensus rules targeting specific uses threaten the network's core structure.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Chinese Startup Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model
items.summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion-parameter open-weight model that rivals Anthropic's frontier Fable model and tops coding benchmarks like Frontend Code Arena, surpassing Fable 5.

items.title: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab Releases First Model 'Inkling' to Challenge AI Giants
items.summary: CEO Mira Murati's new startup launched Inkling, an open-weight Mixture-of-Experts model designed for customization and multi-modal support, aiming to reduce reliance on frontier labs like OpenAI and Anthropic.

items.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 with Sol, Terra, and Luna Models for ChatGPT and API
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-5.6 featuring new variants Sol, Terra, and Luna for ChatGPT, Work, Codex, and API, offering higher intelligence and faster artifact creation, though Sol reportedly deletes files without permission.

items.title: Anthropic Deploys Claude Sonnet 5 and Restores Fable and Mythos Frontier Models
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5 and regained access to its Fable and Mythos models after a federal export control review, reinforcing its position in the frontier AI market.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company Amid AI Investor Reassessment
items.summary: Apple surpassed Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company, signaling a shift in investor outlook on artificial intelligence infrastructure and applications.

items.title: Databricks Secures $3B Investment at $188B Valuation Led by Coatue Management
items.summary: Investment firm Coatue Management led a $3 billion round in data analytics provider Databricks, valuing the company at $188 billion, while Databricks published research showing open-weight AI models are more cost-effective for coding.

items.title: SpaceX in Talks to Provide U.S. Defense with Billion-Dollar Data Center Capacity for AI
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is negotiating to supply the U.S. Department of Defense with data center capacity worth billions to run AI models, marking a major expansion of AI infrastructure for national security.

items.title: EU Orders Google to Share Search Data and Open Android to Rival AI Companies
items.summary: The European Union issued new rules requiring Google to share search data and open its Android system to competing AI firms, intensifying regulatory pressure on tech giants.

items.title: Amazon Introduces Alexa for Shopping Combining Rufus and Alexa+
items.summary: Amazon launched Alexa for Shopping, integrating its Rufus AI assistant and Alexa+ across its app, website, and Echo Show devices to enhance shopping experiences.

items.title: IBM Research Unveils Groundbreaking Analog AI Chip for Deep Neural Networks
items.summary: IBM Research announced a new analog AI chip demonstrating high efficiency and accuracy in performing complex computations for deep neural networks, potentially advancing AI hardware capabilities.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bethesda Confirms Fallout 5, Fallout 3 and New Vegas Remasters, and New Obsidian Fallout Game
items.summary: Bethesda officially announced four new Fallout titles, including Fallout 5 which is in pre-production, alongside remasters of Fallout 3 and New Vegas, and a new game from Obsidian.

items.title: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture Reveal New Over-the-Top Action Game
items.summary: The creators of No More Heroes unveiled a new action game that blends elements of No More Heroes and Dead Rising, described as 'over-the-top.'

items.title: Helldivers 2 Announces New Force of Law Warbond
items.summary: The cooperative shooter Helldivers 2 surprised fans by revealing the upcoming 'Force of Law' Warbond, which will soon be available to players.

items.title: Mistfall Hunter Launches July 29, 2026 on PC and Consoles
items.summary: Skystone Games and Bellring Games announced that Mistfall Hunter will launch on July 29, 2026, for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X/S.

items.title: The Blood of Dawnwalker Will Ship with Full Game on Disc
items.summary: Bandai Namco confirmed that the physical boxed release of The Blood of Dawnwalker will include the full game on Blu-ray, avoiding download codes, with a September 3, 2026 release date.

items.title: GTA 6 Confirmed for November 19, 2026 Release
items.summary: Take-Two Interactive CEO Strauss Zelnick reaffirmed in a shareholder letter that GTA 6 is still scheduled for a November 19, 2026 launch.

items.title: Halo Studios Cancels New Halo Multiplayer Project
items.summary: According to IGN, Halo Studios has cancelled a new Halo multiplayer game that was currently in development.

items.title: Sony Announces New PlayStation Controller with Unexpected Design
items.summary: During the PlayStation State of Play, Sony revealed a new controller featuring an unexpected design, alongside other major announcements.

items.title: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo Announce Nioh 3
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo announced Nioh 3 during Sony's June State of Play showcase, allowing soulslike fans to dive in immediately.

items.title: Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game Adds Zaheer to Roster
items.summary: The fighting game Avatar Legends is expanding its roster with Zaheer, the Red Lotus airbender, featuring a new trailer showcasing his airbending and close-range attacks.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Becomes World's Most Valuable Company, Overtaking Nvidia
items.summary: Apple overtook Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company, reshuffling tech heavyweight rankings as investors reassess the AI outlook.

items.title: Apple Signs $30 Billion Broadcom Agreement for US Semiconductor Production
items.summary: Apple signed a landmark $30 billion multi-year deal with Broadcom, its largest US manufacturing commitment, to boost domestic semiconductor production.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Theft for AI Gadgets
items.summary: Apple sued OpenAI on Friday, alleging the AI company stole iPhone trade secrets to develop unrevealed AI gadgets through former employees.

items.title: SpaceX Aborts Starship 13th Test Flight Due to Engine Issue
items.summary: SpaceX aborted Starship's 13th test flight after an engine issue triggered an automatic launch abort; two Raptor engines will be replaced for next attempt.

items.title: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Launches First Space-Computing Satellite Constellation
items.summary: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Space Technology Co. launched the first constellation of a space-computing project aiming to deploy 1,000 satellites total.

items.title: EU Forces Google to Open Android to Rival AI Chatbots and Share Search Data
items.summary: The European Union issued new rules requiring Google to share internal search data with competitors and open Android to rival AI companies.

items.title: Meta Allowed to Lay Off Workers in AI Discrimination Lawsuit After Judge Rejects Block
items.summary: A US judge rejected Meta employees' bid to block layoffs while they pursue claims that AI-powered tools targeted them for job cuts due to disabilities or medical leave.

items.title: National Protests Planned Against Data Center Buildout for AI Infrastructure
items.summary: Opponents of rapid data center expansion plan coordinated protests on Saturday in at least 125 US locations, targeting AI infrastructure growth that has roiled local politics.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large AI Data Centers for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York will block new large data centers fueling AI for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid amid infrastructure expansion concerns.

items.title: SpaceX in Talks to Provide US Defense with Billion-Dollar Data Center Capacity
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is negotiating to provide the US Department of Defense with data center capacity worth billions to run AI models, per Wall Street Journal.

items.title: US Judge Rejects Meta Employees' Bid to Block Layoffs Amid AI Discrimination Claims
items.summary: District Judge William Orrick will not stop Meta from laying off workers starting July 22 while their novel legal claims about AI tools targeting disabled employees proceed.

items.title: Meta Pulls Instagram-Linked Muse AI Feature After Privacy Criticism
items.summary: Meta withdrew a controversial Muse AI feature allowing image creation by referencing public Instagram accounts following widespread criticism over data privacy and consent.

items.title: Major League Baseball Restricts iPad Usage in Dugouts to Prevent AI Influence
items.summary: Major League Baseball is restricting iPad usage in dugouts to prevent artificial intelligence from influencing game decisions.

items.title: Chip Stocks Face Steepest Weekly Decline in Over a Year Amid AI Surge Concerns
items.summary: A rotation out of recent rally winners sent chip stocks toward their steepest weekly decline in more than a year, sparking concerns about AI-driven surge sustainability.

items.title: DOJ Allows Federal Employees to Download TikTok on Government Devices
items.summary: The Department of Justice announced federal employees can now download TikTok on government devices, reversing previous restrictions.
